data.title: Microbot Medical rises as robotic surgical system succeeds in pivotal trial
data.description:
Shares of Microbot Medical (NASDAQ:MBOT) rose in the premarket on Wednesday after the MedTech firm announced that a pivotal trial for its Liberty endovascular surgical robotic system reached the primary endpoint.

Presenting data from its ACCESS-PVI at a medical conference on interventional radiology, Microbot (NASDAQ:MBOT) said Liberty indicated a 100% success rate in terms of robotic navigation in all 20 cases treated, meeting the study’s main goal.

According to Braintree, Massachusetts-based MBOT, ACCESS-PVI’s operator arm demonstrated a mean 92% relative reduction in radiation exposure, and there were no adverse device events throughout the study’s follow-up period.

The company added that Liberty, an investigational device awaiting FDA’s 510((k)) clearance, indicated a median of three minutes of robotic navigation time to reach target.

“As we shift focus to building our commercial capabilities and preparing for launch, we are confident that LIBERTY® will be well received in the market,” CEO Harel Gadot added.

data.title: Orbit's Electronics Group reports Q1 bookings in excess of $4.7M
data.description:
• Orbit International (OTCPK:ORBT) has announced that its Electronics Group received orders during the Q1 of 2025 totaling in excess of $4.7M.
  • Deliveries for these awards have already commenced and will continue through the second quarter of 2026.
  • "We continue to be confident that in 2025, we will continue to both receive follow-on business for our VPX power supplies and deliver new designs that will expand our reach in the marketplace for this technology." said Mitchell Binder, President and CEO.

data.title: Ronn signs agreement for regional joint venture in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
• Ronn (OTC PINK:RONN) has entered into an agreement with an international intermediary group specializing in Saudia Arabia to form a 50/50 regional joint venture, which would establish a significant operating presence for the company within the Kingdom.
  • "It will establish our manufacturing and production in Saudi Arabia as well as the US where the collaboration with Saudi Arabia is a powerful collaboration at this time," said Ronn Ford, CEO of Ronn.
  • The proposed joint venture in Saudi Arabia is expected to focus on localized manufacturing and regional deployment of Ronn Inc.'s hydrogen technologies, including hydrogen-powered supercars, commercial mobility platforms, hydrogen trucks, and modular fueling infrastructure.
  • Plans for the phase 1 of the agreement currently include the establishment of a regional headquarters in the Kingdom, in collaboration with a group of strategic partners with proven success in rail technology, real estate, transportation, and public-private mega infrastructure. Further details on phase 2 and phase 3 will be disclosed as the joint venture structure and operating scope are finalized, the company said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures fall sharply as China puts 84% tariffs on U.S. products
data.description:
Stock index futures fell sharply on Wednesday as China reportedly slapped 84% tariffs on U.S. products after the Trump administration went ahead with its 104% levy on Chinese goods.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 12 basis points to 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.77%.

In response to the U.S.'s 104% tariffs on Chinese goods, Beijing will raise its tariff on U.S. goods to 84%, effective April 10, the government said on Wednesday, according to media reports.

Another topsy-turvy trading session on Wall Street saw the major averages rally furiously at the open, then steadily give up gains and turn negative, followed by a period of fluctuation. They then decidedly turned lower and eventually ended deep in the red.

"Several triggers drove a sharp turn lower in market sentiment as the day went on. First, we had confirmation from U.S. officials that the additional 50% tariff that Trump threatened China with on Monday would be imposed, dashing any remaining hopes for a last-minute extension before the deadline," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"The market turmoil has shown no sign of letting up over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 edging increasingly close to bear market territory, having now shed -18.9% since its peak in mid-February. Moreover, futures are still pointing towards further losses today,"  Allen said.

Trump on Tuesday also said that the U.S. will soon introduce a "major" tariff on pharmaceutical imports.

"Trump implemented an extraordinary tax increase on US consumers overnight. If the 104% tax increase on goods from China is not reversed, it is likely to push the US into recession more rapidly," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

For years, the U.S. persuaded foreigners to exchange U.S. government promises for valuable goods and services. That exchange is threatened—despite equities falling yesterday, bond yields rose sharply, Donovan added.

The results of $39B, 10-year note auction and the FOMC minutes will land later in the day on Wednesday.

data.title: Altus Power shareholders approve $5/share acquisition by TPG
data.description:
Altus Power (NYSE:AMPS) said Wednesday that shareholders voted to approve the previously announced agreement for the company's acquisition by TPG through its TPG Rise Climate Transition Infrastructure strategy.

Altus Power (NYSE:AMPS) shareholders will receive $5.00 in cash for each share of Altus Power Class A common stock owned immediately prior to the effective time of the merger; the deal valued the company at ~$2.2B including outstanding debt.

By combining TPG Rise Climate’s investment capabilities with Altus Power’s (AMPS) domain expertise in commercial-scale solar, Altus had said it will "be able to scale its operations more rapidly to meet the surging demand for increased power generation."

data.title: BitFuFu reports sequentially higher bitcoin holdings at March-end
data.description:
• Crytocurrency miner BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) announced on Wednesday bitcoin holdings of 1,847 as of March 31, an increase of 47 BTC from the end of February.
  • Shares were -4.82% pre-market at $3.95.
  • The company produced 234 bitcoins for the month, 58 from self-mining operations and 176 for cloud-mining customers.
  • Total hashrate under management stood at 20.6 EH/s as of March-end.

data.title: Aditxt regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Aditxt (NASDAQ:ADTX) said it has received a notice from Nasdaq stating that the firm has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement.
  • Press Release

data.title: Tesla is a best idea pick at Benchmark with catalysts on the way
data.description:
Benchmark reiterated its bullish view on Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and added the electric vehicle stock to its Best Ideas list after watching shares fall sharply this year.

Analyst Mickey Legg said the recent sales declines are significant, but thinks the share price decline is overblown considering the scope of opportunities around the corner.

Legg said the firm's focus is on the release of a new Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) model in Q2, which is expected to turn around the decline in vehicle sales.

"Furthermore, we’re cautiously optimistic about the rollout of Tesla operated robotaxis as a paid service in Austin, Texas, scheduled for June. While the scope of the initial rollout is expected to be limited, we are focused on the rate of expansion of the operation both in Austin and to other cities."

The release of new products, government contracts, and the rollout of full-self driving as a paid service are all seen as potential catalysts this year. Benchmark also highlighted that the Optimus robotics opportunity could be a game changer for Tesla (TSLA) as the company evolves into a broad automation provider. 

Benchmark assigned a price target of $350 to Tesla (TSLA), which was a reduction from the prior PT of $475.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were down 1.1% to $219.50 in premarket trading, after shedding 4.9% on Tuesday.

data.title: LightPath Technologies awarded initial $2.2M IR camera order for U.S. Navy
data.description:
• LightPath Technologies (NASDAQ:LPTH) on Wednesday announced its recently acquired subsidiary G5 Infrared has been awarded an initial $2.2 million engineering development model (EDM) order for infrared cameras by L3Harris Technologies.
  • The contract supports the U.S. Navy's Shipboard Panoramic Electro-Optic/Infrared (SPEIR) Program, with G5's systems scheduled for delivery to L3Harris in the 2025 calendar year.
  • LPTH +1.81% premarket to $1.68.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: JPMorgan Chase's Jamie Dimon says 'Take a breath'
data.description:
The current tariff-induced market swoon doesn't compare with 2008 financial crisis, when the world was massively over-leveraged, JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon said on Wednesday.

To calm the current market turmoil, the government needs to show progress on trade deals, he said.

It's "perfectly reasonable" to say trade practices are unfair, he said during an interview on Fox Business on "Mornings with Maria." But trade agreements don't get done overnight. It will take time to reach trade deals.

"Settle down, take a breath," he advises.

The goal of trade policy changes should be to make Europe a stronger trade partner, to counter China's and Russia's moves to dismantle the post-WWII global order, Dimon said. 

Massive fiscal spending during COVID helped keep the U.S. economy strong. Dimon said slower economic growth was inevitable. He's not yet seeing increased defaults, "but I expect them."

Furthermore, he sees a recession as a "likely outcome."

Still, the U.S. economy is "probably doing the best" of advanced economies, partly because of the government spending during and after COVID.

The U.S. "could be growing by 3% per year" if "we get our act together," he said.

It's hard to say how long it will be before the trade turmoil calms down. "The sooner, the better," Dimon said.

"Don't let this go on too long, because it's causing cumulative damage, especially to the United States," he said.

IPOs are being cancelled, loans are being delayed. It's bad for small businesses, middle-sized companies. He said JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) has lost a couple of bond deals to local banks recently.

"JPMorgan is going to be in fine shape, almost no matter what happens," he commented.

We compete with banks in every country. Hundreds of trillions of dollars per day are moving around the world. Chinese banks are bigger than JPMorgan Chase (JPM) by assets, but not by market cap.

Developing news... check back for updates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
